subject: Mathematics
title: Fibonacci Identities
description: A short lesson to help you apply Fibonacci Identities.

hook.text: Add the squares of two consecutive Fibonacci numbers. You get another Fibonacci number.

buildup.text: This is one of dozens of elegant identities hidden within the Fibonacci sequence.

discovery.text: The sum of the first n Fibonacci numbers equals the (n+2)th Fibonacci number minus one.

twist.text: Every positive integer can be written as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers. This is Zeckendorf's theorem.

climax.text: Fibonacci identities connect to matrix algebra, combinatorics, and even advanced physics.

punchline.text: One simple sequence hides an endless web of identities.

quiz.question: What does Zeckendorf's theorem state?
options: Every positive integer is a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers
Fibonacci numbers alternate odd and even
